evidence_quotes.quote: The game functions 30% offline, but requires you to log in online to access the remainder of the content.
The online servers for the original Monster Hunter on PS2 were shut down on December 31, 2007, only a few months prior and only 31 months after its initial release.
I just wish it was possible offline.
The only way for me to access the main game (+70% of the content) would be to somehow manage my own server, but without any guidance or experience I would not even have known where to start.

paragraphs.text: Hi there! Swedish citizen here. In the end of May 2005 to my birthday, I have got Monster Hunter (release date May 27th 2005 in Europe) for my home console PS2 (PlayStation 2), a game made by Capcom. The game functions 30% offline, but requires you to log in online to access the remainder of the content. This was a glorious time as a young person, due to all the connections I made with people around the world and the experiences it delivered. However, a few years later, in Spring of 2008, I wanted to go online again, to experience the game again. I was aware that I perhaps would be alone, since the sequel had arrived, but to no success. I could not access the online part anymore! Later I quickly found out that the online servers for the original Monster Hunter on PS2 were shut down on December 31, 2007, only a few months prior and only 31 months after its initial release. My disappointment was immeasurable and the day was ruined. I then tried to access the online server somehow in an offline mode, since there is no requirement to go on quests with other people (usually parties of 4), to no avail. The only way for me to access the main game (+70% of the content) would be to somehow manage my own server, but without any guidance or experience I would not even have known where to start. To this day, I cannot access the place I wanted to revisit for finishing the game. I just wish it was possible offline. I will never be able to show my kids the achievements I had there and let them try the experience. I write this, so that future games perhaps may be required to let you play them at least in an offline mode, so that they are not erased from history. Of course only in a feasible manner. An example of unfeasible would be Socom 2: US Navy Seals (another PS2 title), where an offline mode for the online content is not something that makes a lot of sense to access. But what would make sense in these cases, is to upload the server binaries to GitHub or another domain, with some or thorough instructions on how to get a server running. With these words I kindly thank you for your attention to this matter and I will look forward to see any improvements of the law to protect my and many others purchases.
